deck.title: Onboarding Team Sync
deck.tagline: Weekly review · ship updates, experiment health, and what's next.

agenda: Metrics walkthrough — variant balance, funnel, north star

thisWeekShipped.title: Email-First Login A/B test live in prod
thisWeekShipped.detail: iOS 3.23.0 + Android shipped to 2% on April 30. Phased rollout to 80% today, 100% tomorrow.

thisWeekShipped.title: iOS analytics naming collision fixed
thisWeekShipped.detail: PR #4138 — renamed 'login_failed' → 'Login attempt failed' to avoid clobbering legacy table. Merged into release-3.23.0.

thisWeekShipped.title: Analytics Plan finalized with Tristan
thisWeekShipped.detail: 9 events, north star, guardrail metrics, and pre-commit success threshold documented in Confluence.

experiment.jiraTitle: Implement onboarding login A/B test (Flow A & Flow B)
experiment.problem: Barbers who are already registered in a shop download the app and accidentally create a new shop instead of logging into their existing account. They don't realize they already have credentials set up by their shop owner.
experiment.solution: A/B test with two new login flow variants designed to guide existing barbers to log in instead of accidentally signing up.

flowA.name: Flow A — "Welcome back to SQUIRE!"
steps: User opens the app and sees the landing screen with the barbershop background
A login sheet automatically appears with "Welcome back to SQUIRE!" — it cannot be dismissed
User enters their email and password, then taps "Login"
"Forgot password" takes the user to password recovery, then returns to the login sheet
"Don't have an account? Sign up" takes the user to the signup page, then returns to the login sheet

flowB.name: Flow B — "Email-first"
flowB.tagline: Single email entry routes the user to the right path
steps: User sees a new landing: "The essential app for every barber" with "Join for free" and "Login" buttons
Both buttons open a sheet asking for the user's email: "Let's get you started"
Email not found → User redirected to the signup page
Email found, temporary password → "Welcome to SQUIRE, {name}!" — asks for the temporary password from invite email
Email found, existing user → "Welcome back, {name}!" — asks for their password
Temp-password users are asked to set a new password before continuing

rollout.interpretation: Most volume hits Snowflake from May 7 onward. This week's numbers are an instrumentation health check, not a statistical read.

experiment.methodologyNote: There is no in-experiment control group. Comparison vs the current (pre-experiment) login flow is done via before/after analysis at the reporting layer — not within the A/B itself.
experiment.northStar: Did the rate of barbers accidentally creating Indie accounts go down?
experiment.primaryMetric: ACCOUNT_CREATED where account_type = 'indie' AND came_from = 'signup_link', segmented by variant
experiment.successThreshold: Flow B wins if indie signups drop ≥30% without reducing login completion by more than 10%.

metrics.title: Variant balance — is the 50/50 split actually 50/50?
metrics.definition: Distinct user count from LOGIN_EXPERIMENT_ASSIGNED grouped by the variant property (flowA vs flowB).
metrics.whyItMatters: If the split is uneven, every downstream comparison is biased. LaunchDarkly currently shows ~61/39, but per Yurii, RudderStack/Snowflake is our source of truth — we cross-check there.
metrics.watchFor: We expect 50/50 ± a few percent from random assignment noise. Anything outside ~45/55 is suspicious.
metrics.currentRead: LD shows 426 vs 276 (~61/39). Snowflake cross-check pending data pull.

metrics.title: Login completion funnel by variant
metrics.definition: Distinct users at each step: assigned → welcome viewed → email entered (B only) → login succeeded. Conversion rate = login_succeeded ÷ welcome_screen_viewed.
metrics.whyItMatters: Tells us whether either flow is breaking login for real users. A drop here in either variant is a guardrail violation — independent of the false-signup question.
metrics.watchFor: Flow B should not lose more than 10% completion vs Flow A. Bigger gap means the email-first friction outweighs the benefit.
metrics.currentRead: Awaiting Snowflake data. Volumes will be small this week given the 2%→80% rollout — read with caution.

metrics.title: Indie account signups via signup_link
metrics.definition: ACCOUNT_CREATED filtered to account_type = 'indie' AND came_from = 'signup_link', segmented by variant.
metrics.whyItMatters: This is the metric the experiment exists to move. If it drops in Flow B vs Flow A (and vs pre-experiment baseline), we shipped the right thing.
metrics.watchFor: ≥30% reduction in Flow B vs Flow A is the pre-committed success bar. Anything less is a non-decisive result.
metrics.currentRead: 1 row in ACCOUNT_CREATED at the pre-deploy snapshot. Real read needs full-rollout data — earliest meaningful look: next week's sync.

metrics.title: Email check result distribution
metrics.definition: EMAIL_CHECK_RESULT grouped by result: returning · temp_password · new_user. Flow B exclusive.
metrics.whyItMatters: This is the signal the experiment was designed to extract — it tells us who is actually entering through the front door. High temp_password share means we're catching real invited barbers; high new_user share means accidental signups are still leaking through.
metrics.watchFor: Want to see meaningful temp_password share. If new_user dominates, Flow B isn't catching the target population.

metrics.title: Friction signals — where users struggle
metrics.definition: Counts of LOGIN_ATTEMPT_FAILED (by reason), FORGOT_PASSWORD_TAPPED (by from_state), and LOGIN_ABANDONED (by last_step_reached, iOS only).
metrics.whyItMatters: Surfaces UX problems we wouldn't see in completion rates alone. A spike in forgot-password from temp_password users in Flow B = invite email copy needs work.
metrics.watchFor: Flow B should not have a higher forgot-password rate among temp_password users than Flow A users have overall. Login abandonment at the email step in Flow B is also a red flag.

launchDarkly.note: LD dashboard. ~61/39 split — possible SRM. To be cross-checked against RudderStack.

openIssues.title: Sample Ratio Mismatch in LaunchDarkly
openIssues.status: Cross-check vs RudderStack now possible. Per Yurii: Snowflake is source of truth, deprioritize LD investigation.

openIssues.title: Privacy sign-off on user_id property
openIssues.status: Flagged in PR descriptions across iOS + Android rounds. No owner identified yet.

openIssues.status: Held back pending iOS validation. Will push once iOS is stable in prod.
